steps.title: Factor the denominator of the first fraction
steps.content:
The denominator $\pi^{2}-x^{2}$ is a difference of squares:

$$\pi^{2}-x^{2} = (\pi-x)(\pi+x)$$

So the first fraction becomes:
$$\frac{20\pi^{2}}{(\pi-x)(\pi+x)}$$

steps.title: Rewrite with a common denominator
steps.content:
The second fraction has denominator $x+\pi = \pi+x$, which is one of the factors. Multiply the second fraction by $\frac{\pi-x}{\pi-x}$ to get a common denominator:

$$\frac{20\pi^{2}}{(\pi-x)(\pi+x)} - \frac{20\pi(\pi-x)}{(\pi-x)(\pi+x)}$$

$$= \frac{20\pi^{2} - 20\pi(\pi-x)}{(\pi-x)(\pi+x)}$$

steps.title: Simplify the numerator
steps.content:
Expand $20\pi(\pi-x) = 20\pi^{2} - 20\pi x$:

$$20\pi^{2} - (20\pi^{2} - 20\pi x) = 20\pi^{2} - 20\pi^{2} + 20\pi x = 20\pi x$$

The expression becomes:
$$\frac{20\pi x}{(\pi-x)(\pi+x)} = \frac{20\pi x}{\pi^{2}-x^{2}}$$

That matches choice B — B is correct.
